q: A stone tied to a string and whirled in a circle exhibits ________.

When an object is moved slowly and gradually closer to a convex mirror from infinity towards the pole (P), what can we say about the image?
o: Moves from the pole (P) towards the principal focus (F)
Moves from the principal focus (F) towards the pole (P)

q: Which of the following statements is/are correct? A) When a compound consists of a metal and a non-metal, the name or symbol of the metal is written first. B) When a compound consists of a metal and a non-metal, the name or symbol of the non-metal is written last. C) The valency or charges on the ion must balance.
Atomic radius is usually measured in:
A solid cube of wood has a density less than water. When it is placed in water, which of the following statements is correct?
o: The cube will completely sink to the bottom.
The cube will float partially submerged, displacing water equal to its weight.
The cube will float with its entire volume above the water surface.
The cube will remain suspended in the middle of water.

q: The main iron gate of your building rusts over time due to:
Select the correct reason as to why glucose and alcohol do NOT conduct electricity?
o: They do not form ions in solution.

q: Which of the following is the correct reason for NOT being able to hear sound on the Moon?
o: The Moon is very far away from the Earth.

q: A lens with a shorter focal length (f) has ________.
o: Equal power as a lens with a longer focal length (f)

q: Which of the following is NOT an example of inertia?
o: A book sliding on a rough surface and stopping
Dust falling off a carpet when beaten
A ball rolling on a frictionless surface forever
Passenger falling forward when a bus stops suddenly

q: Which of the following analogies did Thomson use to describe his atomic model?
Which crop variety improvement method involves selecting parents with desirable traits and crossing them?
Why do epithelial tissues have very little space between their cells?
o: To create an effective barrier regulating permeability of substances
To enable them to transmit electrical signals

q: Which of the following is/are used in poultry farming?
Which of the following statements correctly explains the variety in the shape and size of cells?
o: The shape and size of all cells remain constant in every organism.
The shape and size of cells are identical in plants and animals.
The shape and size of cells depend only on the availability of water.
The shape and size of cells are related to the specific function they perform.

q: Which of the following contrasting visible characters of garden peas are NOT used by Mendel?
The fundamental property that qualifies magnetic field as a vector quantity is that it possesses both ________.
Which of the following statements accurately describes the characteristic of electrons in an atom?
o: They have the same mass as protons.
They revolve around the nucleus in fixed orbits.
They are located in the nucleus.

q: Fish have hearts with ________ chambers and their blood passes through the heart only ________ in one complete cycle.

What is the dark layer that develops on silver upon its corrosion?

o: they are highly reactive with oxygen and water
they have very low melting points

q: The interior angle of a regular polygon is 156°. How many sides does this polygon have?
What is the percentage of 1/30 of 2/70? (Give the answer correct to two decimal places.)
The volume (in m^3) of a cube, each edge of which is 45 m, is:
Find the sum of the squares of x and y, given that the number 83y547x is divisible by 72.

A hollow cylindrical pipe has an outer radius of 7 cm and an inner radius of 5 cm. If the pipe is 42 cm long and made of metal with a density of 5 g/cm^3, what is the mass of the pipe? (Use π = 22/7 and Density = Mass/Volume)
What is the HCF of 32, 28 and 24?
A trader buys an item for ₹1805 and incurs a loss of 5% while selling it. At what price should he sell the item to make a profit of 5%?

Four years ago, the ratio of a mother's age to her daughter's age was 5:1. After 5 years from now, the ratio of the mother's age to the daughter's age will be 4:1. Find the present ages of the mother and the daughter.
If 2x + 3y = 8 and 4x^2 + 9y^2 = 32, then find the value of 8x^3 + 27y^3.

₹2,037 is divided among Vikas, Tina and Sumit in such a way that if their shares are reduced by ₹23, ₹42 and ₹52 respectively, then their remaining shares are in the ratio 14 : 19 : 15. Find Tina's actual share.
Find the simple interest at 7% per annum on ₹2,000 for the period from 6 March 2023 to 20 April 2023.
If 3/4 of a number is 10% more than 20% of 882, then find the number.

A and B together can complete a piece of work in 12 days, while A and C together can complete the same work in 20 days. If B and C together complete the work in 15 days, find the ratio of the number of days in which A and B alone respectively complete the work.
If 24, 3 and 16 are the fourth proportionals of A, 20 and B respectively, what is the value of A?
A, B and C can each complete a piece of work in 12 days, 24 days and 8 days respectively. If they work together, in how much time (in days) will the work be completed?
A shopkeeper allows a discount of 65% on the marked price of an item and still makes a profit of 82%. Find the ratio of the cost price to the marked price.
